description: The PKCS#11 backend has classify_ck() (secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:75-104) which maps transient PKCS#11 errors (device removed, session closed, token not present) to SecretError::Unavailable and permanent errors (auth failure, bad template) to SecretError::Backend. The TPM2 backend's map_tpm_error() (secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs:183-189) maps ALL tss_esapi errors unconditionally to SecretError::Backend (permanent). This means transient TPM failures — resource manager busy, device temporarily unavailable, context session exhaustion — are reported as permanent errors. Callers following the SecretError contract (retry on Unavailable, abort on Backend) will not retry recoverable TPM failures. Only open_context() (line 177) correctly maps to Unavailable. Fix: classify tss_esapi errors into transient/permanent categories, similar to what classify_ck does for PKCS#11.
title: tpm2: map_tpm_error lacks transient/permanent error classification
